title: Indexing Directives, Canonical Graphs, Hreflang, and Structured Data · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers indexing directives, canonical graphs, hreflang, and structured data into a changed cross-signal search metadata report. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of cross-signal search metadata report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Compare titles and descriptions with visible purpose, uniqueness, language, changed versions, and stakeholder task without universal length claims.
Resolve canonical references and compare redirects, sitemap, internal links, content identity, reachability, and cycles.
Parse robots meta and X-Robots-Tag by product token, response, precedence, and scope while separating crawling from indexing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Title Visible Purpose
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 4158 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile visible purpose, canonicals, index directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and non-claims; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then reorder one origin, retry, channel, context, goroutine, Colly callback, checkpoint, or shutdown event and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare titles and descriptions with visible purpose, uniqueness, language, changed versions, and stakeholder task without universal length claims.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Jsonld Untrusted
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-jsonld-untrusted-1hv9oz" that uses case 3388 to reconcile visible purpose, canonicals, index directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and non-claims. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Jsonld Untrusted.
